titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Department of Athletics, Yale University, Motion Pictures and Audiorecordings Documenting Football
Department of Athletics, Yale University, Motion Pictures and Audiorecordings Documenting Football
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 21, 2020 at 09:50
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Department of Athletics, Yale University, motion pictures and audiorecordings documenting football
did.abstract: The materials consist of 16mm motion picture films, videotapes, and audiocassettes documenting football at Yale. Included are game films for the varsity, freshman, and junior varsity teams, footage of scrimmages, season highlights, a multi-part history of Yale football, and several Yale football-related productions.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 38-B (YRG 38-B), Records documenting specific sports at Yale.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were transferred from the Department of Athletics, 2004-2007; gift of John Kieffer, 2013.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies (designated by a box with a 'U' suffix), or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Department of Athletics, Yale University, Motion Pictures and Audiorecordings Documenting Football (RU 971). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession and thereunder chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of 16mm motion picture films, videotapes, and audiocassettes documenting football at Yale. Included are game films for the varsity, freshman, and junior varsity teams, footage of scrimmages, season highlights, a multi-part history of Yale football, and several Yale football-related productions.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several videotapes from this accession were removed and designated as use copies for films in other accessions.

did.unittitle: Princeton Offensive Highlights, Harvard Offensive Highlights
UConn - Yale offense vs. UConn defense
Cornell - Yale defense vs. Cornell Offense
Princeton - Yale offense vs. Princeton defense
Princeton - Yale defense vs. Princeton defense
The History of Yale Football: From Camp to Cozza
History of Football by Mike Pyle
300th History of Yale Football, tercentennial talk
History of Yale Football, Part I
Celebration of a Legend: Carm Cozza Tribute
Behind the Shield: Yale Connection (Chris Hetherington -Don Martin)
Chris Hetherington and Don Martin -Oakland Raiders TV
Football at Yale -The Story of a Tradition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Films are 16 mm, black and white, silent. Most have strong vinegar smell and measure #2 or 3 on AD strips. Many are badly warped and buckled with deteriorating film base.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives.

did.unittitle: Sports TV Presents All-American Game of the Week with Tom Harman [Yale vs. Princeton?]

odd.head: General
odd.p: Filmed by George Weber; music by Yale Band; narrated by Jordan Olivar, football coach.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Narrated by Jordan Olivar, football coach.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Filmed by George Weber; music by Yale Band.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Title: Alumni Board presents Yale Football Ivy League Highlights 1959; narrated by head coach Jordan Olivar.

did.unittitle: Telenews highlights of Harvard (game filmed by James M. Banner '30)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Title: Ivy League Championship Football Highlights 1960; narrated by Jordan Olivar; filmed by George Weber; music by Yale Band and Glee Club.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Narrated by Jordan Olivar; music by Yale Band and Glee Club; filmed by George Weber.

did.unittitle: Season Highlights (narrated by Yale Head Coach John Pont)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Narrated by Yale Head Coach Carmen Cozza.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Narrated by Head Coach Carm Cozza.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Reel also contains film of the Yale-Harvard boat race, probably spring 1930 or 1931.
